ucs.title: [LLM] Midnight Blizzard OAuth illicit-consent → Graph/EXO service-principal mailbox harvesting
ucs.description: Detects APT29's signature post-SolarWinds cloud tradecraft (used in the 2024 Microsoft corporate and HPE Aruba intrusions): a high-privilege OAuth consent grant to a third-party app for Mail.* / full_access_as_app / Directory.* scopes, followed by service-principal sign-ins to Exchange Online or Microsoft Graph that read/search corporate mailboxes. APT29 prefers app-context (not user-context) mailbox access because it bypasses MFA and conditional access entirely.
ucs.splunk:
`azure_ad_audit` operationName IN ("Consent to application","Add app role assignment to service principal","Add delegated permission grant","Add OAuth2PermissionGrant")
| spath path=properties.targetResources{}.displayName output=app_name
| spath path=properties.targetResources{}.id output=app_id
| spath path=properties.targetResources{}.modifiedProperties{}.newValue output=scopes
| spath path=properties.initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName output=initiator
| spath path=properties.initiatedBy.user.ipAddress output=initiator_ip
| eval scopes_str=mvjoin(scopes,",")
| search scopes_str IN ("*Mail.Read*","*Mail.ReadWrite*","*Mail.Send*","*full_access_as_app*","*Directory.ReadWrite.All*","*MailboxSettings*")
| eval consent_time=_time
| join type=inner app_id [
    search `o365_management_activity` Workload=Exchange Operation IN (MailItemsAccessed,Send,SearchQueryInitiatedExchange,UpdateInboxRules) (UserType=Application OR UserType=ServicePrincipal)
    | rename AppId as app_id
    | stats dc(MailboxOwnerUPN) as mailboxes_touched, values(Operation) as ops, min(_time) as first_app_access by app_id
    | where mailboxes_touched >= 1
  ]
| eval delay_min=round((first_app_access-consent_time)/60,1)
| table consent_time, initiator, initiator_ip, app_name, app_id, scopes_str, first_app_access, delay_min, mailboxes_touched, ops
| sort - consent_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let HighRiskScopes = dynamic(["Mail.Read","Mail.ReadWrite","Mail.Send","MailboxSettings.ReadWrite","full_access_as_app","Directory.Read.All","Directory.ReadWrite.All"]);
let ConsentGrants = CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in~ ("Consent to application.","Add delegated permission grant.","Add app role assignment to service principal.","Add OAuth2PermissionGrant.")
    | extend RawProps = tostring(RawEventData)
    | where RawProps has_any (HighRiskScopes)                                  // narrows to dangerous scopes (Mail.*, Directory.*, full_access_as_app)
    | extend AppName = tostring(ObjectName), AppId = tostring(ObjectId)
    | project ConsentTime = Timestamp, ConsentingUser = AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId,
              AppName, AppId, IPAddress, CountryCode, ActionType, RawProps;
ConsentGrants
| join kind=inner (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where TokenIssuerType =~ "AzureAD"
    | where ResourceDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange Online","Microsoft Graph")
    | summarize SpnSignIns = count(),
                FirstSpnAuth = min(Timestamp),
                LastSpnAuth  = max(Timestamp),
                Resources    = make_set(ResourceDisplayName),
                SourceIPs    = make_set(IPAddress, 25)
                by ApplicationId
  ) on $left.AppId == $right.ApplicationId
| where FirstSpnAuth >= ConsentTime - 1h                                       // app activated near consent (rules out legacy apps)
| extend MinutesToFirstAuth = datetime_diff('minute', FirstSpnAuth, ConsentTime)
| project ConsentTime, ConsentingUser, IPAddress, CountryCode, AppName, AppId,
          MinutesToFirstAuth, SpnSignIns, Resources, SourceIPs, RawProps
| order by ConsentTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let HighRiskScopes = dynamic(["Mail.Read","Mail.ReadWrite","Mail.Send","MailboxSettings.ReadWrite","full_access_as_app","Directory.Read.All","Directory.ReadWrite.All"]);
let ConsentGrants = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OperationName in ("Consent to application","Add delegated permission grant","Add app role assignment to service principal","Add OAuth2PermissionGrant")
    | extend AppName    = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
             AppId      = tostring(TargetResources[0].id),
             ScopesRaw  = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties),
             Initiator  = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             InitiatorIp = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress)
    | where ScopesRaw has_any (HighRiskScopes)
    | project ConsentTime = TimeGenerated, OperationName, Initiator, InitiatorIp, AppName, AppId, ScopesRaw;
let AppMailUse = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where Operation in~ ("MailItemsAccessed","Send","SearchQueryInitiatedExchange","New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule")
    | where UserType in ("Application","ServicePrincipal")
    | extend AppId = tostring(parse_json(tostring(EventData)).AppId)
    | summarize MailboxesTouched = dcount(MailboxOwnerUPN),
                FirstAppAccess  = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastAppAccess   = max(TimeGenerated),
                Ops             = make_set(Operation),
                MailboxSample   = make_set(MailboxOwnerUPN, 25)
                by AppId;
ConsentGrants
| join kind=inner AppMailUse on AppId
| where FirstAppAccess >= ConsentTime - 1h                                     // app starts harvesting close to consent
| extend MinutesToFirstAccess = datetime_diff('minute', FirstAppAccess, ConsentTime)
| project ConsentTime, Initiator, InitiatorIp, AppName, AppId, ScopesRaw,
          MinutesToFirstAccess, MailboxesTouched, Ops, MailboxSample, LastAppAccess
| order by ConsentTime desc
ucs.rationale: This is the exact kill chain Microsoft documented for Midnight Blizzard's Jan 2024 corporate breach (MSRC blog 'Midnight Blizzard: Guidance for responders on nation-state attack') and the HPE Aruba intrusion. Generic 'OAuth consent' rules alert on every legitimate SaaS adoption — this rule restricts to mail-grade scopes AND requires the app to actually start touching mailboxes within ~1 hour of gr

ucs.title: [LLM] APT29 residential-proxy password spray against legacy auth → successful sign-in pivot
ucs.description: Detects APT29 / Midnight Blizzard's documented initial-access pattern (Microsoft corporate breach 2024, multiple government/think-tank intrusions): low-and-slow password spraying from residential-proxy / TOR exit IPs against legacy authentication endpoints (IMAP, POP, SMTP AUTH, ActiveSync, 'Other clients') to bypass MFA, then a successful interactive or non-interactive sign-in from the same source IP within hours. SVR specifically targets legacy auth because it ignores Conditional Access in many tenants.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true
    count as fail_count,
    dc(Authentication.user) as target_users,
    values(Authentication.user) as failed_users,
    min(_time) as first_fail,
    max(_time) as last_fail
  from datamodel=Authentication
  where Authentication.action=failure
    AND Authentication.signature_id IN ("50053","50126","50055","50056","50064","50076")
    AND (Authentication.app IN ("Other clients","Exchange ActiveSync","IMAP4","POP3","SMTP","Authenticated SMTP") OR Authentication.authentication_method="legacy")
  by Authentication.src _time span=1h
| rename Authentication.src as src
| where target_users >= 20 AND fail_count >= 50
| join type=inner src [
    | tstats summariesonly=true
        count as success_count,
        values(Authentication.user) as success_user,
        values(Authentication.app) as success_app,
        values(Authentication.user_agent) as success_ua,
        min(_time) as success_time
      from datamodel=Authentication
      where Authentication.action=success
        AND Authentication.signature_id="0"
      by Authentication.src Authentication.user
    | rename Authentication.src as src Authentication.user as authd_user
  ]
| where success_time > first_fail AND success_time < (last_fail + 21600)
| eval pivot_delay_min=round((success_time-last_fail)/60,1)
| table first_fail last_fail src target_users fail_count authd_user success_app success_ua pivot_delay_min success_time
| sort - success_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let SprayWindow = 1h;
let MinUsersSprayed = 20;
let MinFailures = 50;
let PostSprayWindow = 6h;
let LegacyAuth = dynamic(["Other clients","Exchange ActiveSync","IMAP4","POP3","SMTP","Authenticated SMTP"]);
let SprayFailureCodes = dynamic([50053, 50126, 50055, 50056, 50064, 50076]);   // pwd-fail/locked/MFA-required
let SprayIPs = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ErrorCode in (SprayFailureCodes)
    | where ClientAppUsed in (LegacyAuth) or IsInteractive == false
    | summarize TargetUsers = dcount(AccountUpn),
                FailCount  = count(),
                FailedUsers = make_set(AccountUpn, 50),
                Apps       = make_set(Application),
                FirstFail  = min(Timestamp),
                LastFail   = max(Timestamp)
                by IPAddress, Country, bin(Timestamp, SprayWindow)
    | where TargetUsers >= MinUsersSprayed and FailCount >= MinFailures;
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ErrorCode == 0                                                          // successful auth
| join kind=inner (SprayIPs | project IPAddress, Country, FirstFail, LastFail, TargetUsers, FailCount, FailedUsers) on IPAddress
| where Timestamp between (FirstFail .. LastFail + PostSprayWindow)
| extend SuccessAfterSprayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, LastFail)
| project SuccessTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, City,
          Application, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent, IsAnonymousProxy,
          RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ConditionalAccessStatus,
          TargetUsers, FailCount, SprayStart = FirstFail, SprayEnd = LastFail,
          SuccessAfterSprayMin
| order by SuccessTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let SprayWindow = 1h;
let MinUsersSprayed = 20;
let MinFailures = 50;
let PostSprayWindow = 6h;
let LegacyAuth = dynamic(["Other clients","Exchange ActiveSync","IMAP4","POP3","SMTP","Authenticated SMTP"]);
let SprayFailureCodes = dynamic([50053, 50126, 50055, 50056, 50064, 50076]);
let AllSignIns = union isfuzzy=true
    (SigninLogs                       | extend SrcTbl = "Interactive"),
    (AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs  | extend SrcTbl = "NonInteractive");
let SprayIPs = AllSignIns
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ResultType in (SprayFailureCodes)
    | where ClientAppUsed in (LegacyAuth) or IsInteractive == false
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(tostring(LocationDetails)).countryOrRegion)
    | summarize TargetUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
                FailCount  = count(),
                FailedUsers = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 50),
                Apps       = make_set(AppDisplayName),
                FirstFail  = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastFail   = max(TimeGenerated)
                by IPAddress, Country, bin(TimeGenerated, SprayWindow)
    | where TargetUsers >= MinUsersSprayed and FailCount >= MinFailures;
AllSignIns
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ResultType == 0
| join kind=inner (SprayIPs | project IPAddress, Country, FirstFail, LastFail, TargetUsers, FailCount, FailedUsers) on IPAddress
| where TimeGenerated between (FirstFail .. LastFail + PostSprayWindow)
| extend SignInCountry = tostring(parse_json(tostring(LocationDetails)).countryOrRegion)
| extend SuccessAfterSprayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, LastFail)
| project SuccessTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, SignInCountry,
          AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ConditionalAccessStatus,
          TargetUsers, FailCount, SprayStart = FirstFail, SprayEnd = LastFail, SuccessAfterSprayMin
| order by SuccessTime desc
ucs.rationale: APT29 is uniquely associated with this exact tradecraft pattern by Microsoft (Midnight Blizzard 2024 corporate intrusion writeup) and CISA AA23-347A: spray slowly from residential proxies against legacy auth endpoints to evade per-IP throttling AND avoid MFA. Generic password-spray rules alert on the spray itself (millions of FPs from internet noise); this rule only fires when a spray IP also land
